pre_test.question: What is the primary role of CLIP in a text-to-image system?

options.a: It generates the final image pixels directly from text
options.b: It translates text and images into a shared language so they can be compared
options.c: It removes noise from images during the generation process
options.d: It compresses images to reduce file size

pre_test.question: Why does the same text prompt produce different images each time?

options.a: The model has bugs that cause random behavior
options.b: The process starts from random noise, and different starting noise leads to different valid outputs
options.c: The text is interpreted differently each time

pre_test.question: What mechanism allows the text prompt to influence which parts of the image get modified?

options.a: The text is converted to pixels and overlaid on the image
options.b: A random selection process picks image regions to modify
options.c: Cross-attention lets each image region check the text to decide what to generate
options.d: The entire image is regenerated from scratch at each step

pre_test.question: A generated image shows the right colors but wrong shapes. What is the most likely cause?

options.b: The denoiser lacks spatial awareness (no convolutional layers)
options.c: The noise schedule is too aggressive
options.d: The image resolution is too low

pre_test.question: Which factor has the biggest impact on the cost of generating an image?

options.a: The length of the text prompt
options.b: The number of denoising steps (each requires a full model pass)
options.c: The color palette of the output image
options.d: The file format of the saved image

post_test.question: Your team says they 'fine-tuned CLIP for your product catalog.' What did they actually do?

options.a: They trained a model to generate product images from descriptions
options.b: They trained a model to place product photos and descriptions near each other in a shared embedding space
options.c: They built a search engine using keyword matching
options.d: They compressed the product database for faster retrieval

post_test.question: A client complains that 'the AI gives inconsistent results.' How do you explain this?

options.a: The model needs to be retrained with more data
options.b: This is by design -- different random seeds produce different valid interpretations, like different artists painting the same scene
options.c: There is a bug in the deployment pipeline
options.d: The model is too small and needs more parameters

post_test.question: Your vendor's model generates 'a silver watch on a wooden table' but the watch looks wooden. What went wrong?

options.a: The model doesn't understand the word 'silver'
options.b: The training data didn't include enough watches
options.c: Cross-attention is bleeding -- the material attributes are being applied to the wrong objects
options.d: The image resolution is too low to show metallic textures

post_test.question: Your team's custom model produces colorful but blobby images. What architectural change would you suggest?

options.b: Replace the fully-connected denoiser with a convolutional one (UNet) for spatial awareness
options.d: Increase the number of denoising steps

post_test.question: Your company wants to cut image generation costs by 50%. Which approach would be most effective?

options.b: Reduce the number of denoising steps (e.g., from 50 to 25) or use a distilled model
options.c: Generate smaller images and upscale them afterward
options.d: Switch to a different programming language
